title: henckelia pleuropogon
intro: Henckelia pleuropogon, also known as Indian gentian, is a plant species belonging to the Gentianaceae family, commonly found in the Himalayan regions. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. Its health benefits include treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as providing relief from digestive problems and skin conditions. Henckelia pleuropogon can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine preparations to promote overall well-being and combat various health ailments.
